title: athrotaxis selaginoides
intro: Athrotaxis selaginoides, also known as the King Billy pine, is a coniferous tree native to the mountainous regions of Tasmania and Victoria in Australia. The resin of Athrotaxis selaginoides has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Athrotaxis selaginoides have been reported to provide health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as antiseptic and antifungal properties. Athrotaxis selaginoides is typically used in herbal preparations, including essential oils, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
